premise: I drained my $40,000 retirement fund to care for my sister during her final year of ovarian cancer, and her will left her $390,000 lake house equally to me and her husband. My brother-in-law forged my signature on a partition sale, pocketed the entire $390,000 in cash, and told my niece I’d abandoned the family. I hired a forensic title examiner, proved the forgery, and won a $340,000 civil judgment — when he couldn’t pay, I accepted the deed to his deceased parents’ century farm in settlement. I now operate a free summer camp for grieving children there, and charge his family full price to set foot on their ancestral land.
